title: Heptapleurum carrii
intro: Heptapleurum carrii, also known as the 'Carrie's tree fern', is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal properties, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Heptapleurum carrii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and lignans, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
